# Microbiome Intervention Analysis using `mbtransfer`

`mbtransfer` is an R package for modeling and inference of microbial
communities under dynamic environmental interventions. It supports
simulation of hypothetical community trajectories under user-specified
perturbations and can be used to select for taxa that are significantly
impacted by this change in a statistically rigorous sense. It
accomplishes this by learning a separate transfer function model for
each taxon, relating its changes in abundance to its immediate community
and environmental context. The fitted models can be used to measure the
significance of the intervention on individual taxa,

![](https://krisrs1128.github.io/mbtransfer/articles/diet_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-14-1.png)

(larger mirror statistics correspond to stronger effects) and to
simulate trajectory differences between counterfactual treatments,

![](https://krisrs1128.github.io/mbtransfer/articles/diet_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-18-1.png)

You can read more about the methodology in our
[preprint]((https://arxiv.org/abs/2306.06364)) and can browse complete
examples in the [package
documentation](https://krisrs1128.github.io/mbtransfer).

## Installation

You can install `mbtransfer` from this repository using:

``` r

#install.packages("devtools")
devtools::install_github("krisrs1128/mbtransfer")
```

## Help

We welcome questions and comments about the package! You can reach us
either through [github](https://github.com/krisrs1128/mbtransfer/issues)
or [email](https://krisrs1128.github.io/LSLab/_includes/contact).

# Package index

## All functions

- [`append_interactions()`](append_interactions.md) : Add Interactions
  to Predictor Matrix

- [`consistency_mirror()`](consistency_mirror.md) : Mirror Statistics
  from a Single Split

- [`consistency_mirror_multisplit()`](consistency_mirror_multisplit.md)
  : Compute Mirrors across Splits

- [`counterfactual_ts()`](counterfactual_ts.md) : Generate
  Counterfactual versions of a ts_inter object

- [`interaction_hm()`](interaction_hm.md) : Visualize Time Series
  Heatmaps

- [`interaction_search()`](interaction_search.md) : Search for Candidate
  Interactions

- [`mbtransfer()`](mbtransfer.md) :

  Transfer Function Model for `ts_inter` objects

- [`mbtransfer_model-class`](mbtransfer_model-class.md) : Transfer
  Function Model Class

- [`mbtransfer_predict()`](mbtransfer_predict.md) :

  Prediction method for `mbtransfer` objects

- [`model_predict_single()`](model_predict_single.md) : Prediction for a
  Single Subject

- [`model_predict_step()`](model_predict_step.md) : Predictions for a
  single timepoint and subject

- [`patchify_df()`](patchify_df.md) : Sliding Windows for a ts_inter
  object

- [`patchify_single()`](patchify_single.md) : Sliding Windows for a
  ts_inter_single object

- [`patchify_single_df()`](patchify_single_df.md) :

  Convert `patchify_single` output into a `data.frame`

- [`pd_effects()`](pd_effects.md) : Counterfactual Partial Dependence
  Effects

- [`pd_splits()`](pd_splits.md) : Mirror Splits from Partial Dependence

- [`pivot_ts()`](pivot_ts.md) : Joined ts dfs

- [`predict(`*`<mbtransfer_model>`*`)`](predict-mbtransfer_model-method.md)
  : Prediction method for mbtransfer models

- [`predictor_names()`](predictor_names.md) : Create uniform column
  names for patchified df

- [`predictors()`](predictors.md) : Generate a Predictor Matrix

- [`pulses()`](pulses.md) : Hypothetical Pulse Interventions

- [`read_figshare_csv()`](read_figshare_csv.md) : Read a CSV file hosted
  on Figshare

- [`replace_inter_()`](replace_inter_.md) : ts is a single element of a
  ts_inter class

- [`sample_ts()`](sample_ts.md) :

  Randomly subset a `ts_inter` object

- [`select_taxa()`](select_taxa.md) : Significant Taxa using Mirror
  Statistics

- [`sim_ts`](sim_ts.md) : Simulated Time Series

- [`steps()`](steps.md) : Hypothetical Step Interventions

- [`subset_values()`](subset_values.md) : Subset values of a ts object

- [`ts_from_dfs()`](ts_from_dfs.md) :

  Build a `ts`

- [`ts_inter-class`](ts_inter-class.md) : An S4 class representing a
  collection of time series under environmental shifts

- [`ts_inter_single-class`](ts_inter_single-class.md) : An S4 class to
  represent a single subject's series
